concrete_observations.observation: Output was valid JSON-shaped and not truncated, but failed schema because a thesis used supports=analogue, which is not allowed by the AS1 thesis.supports enum.
Output passed schema and reached 0.9286 deterministic must-point recall, but relevance accuracy was only 0.5833, suggesting relevance labels need review or model calibration.
Output passed schema and reached 0.9286 deterministic must-point recall, but relevance accuracy was only 0.4167, again showing relevance grading sensitivity.
Output failed schema because a thesis used supports=analogue. This mirrors the Claude issue and suggests the schema may be stricter than the natural task vocabulary.

deterministic_scorer_blind_spots: Must-point recall uses token overlap and can over-credit verbose outputs that mention the right concepts without making the right request-specific point.
The scorer can under-credit concise but semantically correct paraphrases.
Relevance accuracy may be too brittle if adjacent analogues can reasonably be high or medium depending on reviewer policy.
Forbidden-claim checks are exact or near-exact and will miss subtle overstatement.
The scorer does not yet judge whether evidence notes are truly supported by article text.

schema_review_items.recommendation: Consider adding analogue to thesis.supports, or clarify in the prompt that analogue belongs only in support_type while thesis.supports must describe confirm/weaken/nuance/risk/background.
Expert should review whether several medium analogue articles should allow high as acceptable, or whether exact-match relevance scoring should become weighted.

proposed_calibration_outputs.name: good_request_specific
proposed_calibration_outputs.purpose: Should pass: correct relevance labels, concise request-specific points, no overstatement, distractor kept irrelevant.

proposed_calibration_outputs.name: generic_article_recaps
proposed_calibration_outputs.purpose: Should fail or score low: schema-valid summaries that merely recap each article without tying back to Avito ND.

proposed_calibration_outputs.name: analogue_overstated_as_proof
proposed_calibration_outputs.purpose: Should fail forbidden/overstatement checks: treats portal economics and CRM analogues as proof of developer CPA.

proposed_calibration_outputs.name: distractor_promoted
proposed_calibration_outputs.purpose: Should fail distractor handling: marks the generic AI marketing article as high relevance with confirming theses.

proposed_calibration_outputs.name: schema_valid_semantically_weak
proposed_calibration_outputs.purpose: Should expose deterministic scorer limits: correct IDs and enums but vague theses with weak grounding.

expansion_decision.reason: Do not expand to other cases until expert review decides the thesis.supports enum issue and whether deterministic scoring needs an LLM judge before broader runs.
